rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Do not add new architecture elements when resolving AGREE diagnostics; only repair existing constructs.
generic_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace and resolve the first invalid identifier on the same line.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts to avoid resolution errors.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output feature bindings instead.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must reference valid identifiers that are either AADL features or declared auxiliary variables.
Assign statements in implementation scopes should target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing references by introducing new component ports or connections in the architecture.
Do not add new annex content or modify component type contracts when repairing implementation-level AGREE diagnostics.
